findings.title: Local-sort retry cleanup change still has no focused regression coverage
findings.why: This patch changes the subtask failure cleanup sequence for local sort and turns `CleanupAllLocalEngines` into a fire-and-forget path, but the changed retry/cleanup behavior is still untested. For a partial-failure path that is meant to keep retries and restarts safe, the current evidence is only static reasoning.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: A later refactor can easily break the error cleanup ordering or the best-effort cleanup invocation without CI catching it, leaving stale local engines behind after a failed import and making retry/restart behavior less predictable for operators.
findings.evidence: The diff changes only production code in `pkg/dxf/importinto/task_executor.go`, `pkg/lightning/backend/local/engine_mgr.go`, and `pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go`. A repository search under `pkg/**/*_test.go` finds no test referencing `CleanupAllLocalEngines`, and no import-into test exercises the local-sort `RunSubtask` failure branch that now defers cleanup before opening engines. Make sure retry/cancel path is covered.
findings.change_request: Add UT for it: cover a local-sort `RunSubtask` failure after engines are opened, and assert the cleanup path runs deterministically on error. Please add at least one negative case that verifies retries start from a cleaned engine state, plus a case where cleanup itself reports an error so the logging-only behavior remains intentional.
